So, our last exercise is a pretty simple one, but yields pretty interesting results when you actually run clients through it. So this is called the million dollar bet, and it's going to play a big part in your perfect for you if close. So this is all about getting absolutely uncompromisingly clear about who is a perfect fit for your product or service. So there's no compromising here. There's no good for, it's right for you if this is perfect for you if, and we're getting to that place of the perfect fit by literally, well not literally, unless you're pretty hardcore like that, but by visualizing that you're placing a bet, you're placing a million dollars, you're writing a check, and I've actually made clients write out a million dollar check to just get in that mindset of what it would be like. I've had them write it out and show it to me on the zoom camera and say, this is what is at stake if my client doesn't get the results I'm promising in this program. So if you have to bet on customer success, if you have to bet a million dollars on your prospect on your three dimensional avatar achieving the things and outcomes that you say your product can and will get them, then who are they? What have they already tried and failed at? How deep are they in that space or industry or along that path of trying to get that result? So sometimes having failed at a few things first is actually a prerequisite for success later. What have they already accomplished? So this ties back into those points of exclusive empowerment. What have they accomplished in the past that empowers them to even strive to achieve the results you talked about in your program? And what are they currently doing in their day to day to demonstrate commitment to solving that problem? It's one thing to say they want it, but what are they doing in actuality? How can you reflect back the ways that they are committed to this outcome to really show them, look, you already are on this track. You already are investing time, energy and money in creating this outcome or overcoming this problem. Obviously, if you're placing the bet on someone, you want observable commitment towards solving it already. And the thing is a lot of people won't even realize how committed they are until you reflect it to them. So that's why we're trying to nail that part of this equation down. And then what else are they doing that gives you confidence in their ability to succeed? So what other little, sometimes innocuous things would you be looking for in someone that you're placing such a large bet on? And what would be their attitudinal or emotional state that gives you confidence? So what is it about the way they present themselves or about the way they're talking about their problems or about the way they're researching solutions that would give you confidence in them actually getting the results in your program? So those are just a handful of things I look for in running the million dollar bet exercise. And of course, take this as deep as you want, call some audibles if you're doing this with a client or with your team and just really try to pinpoint the characteristics and qualifications of someone who wouldn't only be a good fit for your product, but someone who you would be willing to bet a large amount of money on in achieving success with your product. So that's the million dollar bet.
